title: Sync daily-heart-rate-variability into new daily_hrv table
description:
Add a cursor-walked sync for daily-heart-rate-variability — the highest-value passive signal for the body-sim 'unusual day' detector (illness/alcohol/sleep-debt all drop HRV 20-50% from baseline).

New table daily_hrv:
- date (PK)
- avg_hrv_ms (REAL, from averageHeartRateVariabilityMilliseconds)
- deep_sleep_rmssd_ms (REAL, from deepSleepRootMeanSquareOfSuccessiveDifferencesMilliseconds)
- non_rem_hr_bpm (INTEGER, from nonRemHeartRateBeatsPerMinute — note: returned as string<int64>, cast at parse)
- entropy (REAL)
- source, external_id

New client method GoogleHealthClient.list_daily_hrv. Filter: daily_heart_rate_variability.date >= 'YYYY-MM-DD'. Actions supported: list, reconcile (no rollup). 90-day backfill default; cursor-walk on date thereafter.

New _sync_daily_hrv in HealthSyncService following the same pattern as _sync_resting_hr. Wire into sync_all.

Tests: parser test against captured live payload (see /tmp/probe2_report.json), integration test using TestClient with mocked HTTP.

Full schema sketch: docs/health-data-discovery-pixel-watch.md §2.
close_reason: New daily_hrv table + list_daily_hrv parser + _sync_daily_hrv cursor-walk + MCP get_daily_hrv tool. Live verification: 22 rows synced, avg HRV 25-65 ms, deep-sleep RMSSD 21-58 ms, all physiologically sane. 5 new tests pass; all 177 non-body_sim tests pass overall. Commit 0526d5d.

title: Sync sleep stages + per-night summary into sleep_sessions
description:
Extend _sync_sleep in foodlog/services/health_sync.py to parse fields already on the v4 sleep payload we fetch but currently discard.

Add columns to sleep_sessions:
- sleep_type (STAGES/CLASSIC/UNSPECIFIED) — from sleep.type
- nap (bool) — from sleep.metadata.nap
- stages_status — from sleep.metadata.stagesStatus
- awake_min, light_min, deep_min, rem_min, asleep_min, restless_min — from sleep.summary.stagesSummary
- in_period_min — from sleep.summary.minutesInSleepPeriod

Zero new HTTP cost (same payload, more fields read). Add a SleepSessionRow extension in google_health.py to carry the new fields. Add migration. Add tests covering: STAGES session with all 4 stage types, CLASSIC session with no stages, a session with stagesStatus != SUCCEEDED.

Full schema sketch: docs/health-data-discovery-pixel-watch.md §1.

Acceptance criteria:
- New columns populated for last 30 days of synced sleep
- Tests pass
- Dashboard surfaces don't regress (no UI change required, just storage)
notes: Merged from superseded foodlog-taq (2026-05-19 brainstorm bead). Additional scope absorbed: update MCP get_sleep tool response schema to include stages when present. Also: foodlog-taq predicted that the body-sim phase-1 daily-rollup table will absorb new columns (sleep_rem_h, sleep_deep_h, sleep_light_h, sleep_awake_h) transparently — when that daily-rollup table is built, ensure the column names align with the v4 schema names used here.
close_reason: Implemented + deployed + verified. sleep_sessions extended with 10 nullable columns (sleep_type/nap/stages_status + per-stage minute totals + asleep/in_period totals). Live backfill: 20 sessions in last 30 days, 19 STAGES with full breakdown. New ensure_columns helper handles the schema migration. MCP get_sleep exposes new fields. 4 new tests (parser, sync, MCP, migration helper). All 172 non-body_sim tests pass. Commit 00de15a. Unblocks foodlog-usa (doc fix).

title: Dashboard hides Weight card when body-fat row ties measured_at
description:
When the BodyComposition table contains both a weight row and a body-fat row at the same measured_at (which happens on a barefoot weigh-in — Renpho transmits weight + body fat as two separate Google Health data points with the same physicalTime), the dashboard's latest_body query in foodlog/api/routers/dashboard.py:184-185 returns the body-fat row first (weight_kg=None) because ORDER BY measured_at DESC has no tiebreaker. The check 'if latest_body and latest_body.weight_kg' then fails, weight_view becomes None, and the Weight card silently disappears from the dashboard.

Confirmed against live DB on 2026-05-05: barefoot weigh-in at 2026-05-04 10:56:52 produced body-fat row with weight_kg=None and weight row with weight_kg=82.85; LIMIT 1 returns the body-fat row.

Same shape bug in week_ago lookup at dashboard.py:188-191 — kills the weekly delta if the boundary row happens to be body-fat.

The doc claim in HEALTH_DATA.md that 'Renpho → Fitbit drops body fat' is also stale — body fat IS coming through now when the user weighs barefoot. Update doc as part of fix.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Weight card renders on dashboard whenever a weight reading exists, regardless of co-occurring body-fat row at same measured_at. 2) Body-fat percentage shown on the card when a body-fat row exists at the same measured_at. 3) Weekly delta computed correctly even when boundary row is body-fat. 4) Regression test reproduces the original bug shape (two rows tied on measured_at, weight card still renders). 5) HEALTH_DATA.md updated to reflect that body fat does propagate via Fitbit when measured.
close_reason: Filtered latest_body and week_ago queries to weight_kg IS NOT NULL; added sibling-row body-fat lookup; updated HEALTH_DATA.md. Two regression tests in tests/test_dashboard.py. Verified live: dashboard now reports weight=82.85 kg, body_fat=20.6% from 2026-05-04 weigh-in.

title: Correct doc/HEALTH_DATA.md sleep-stages claims (currently wrong)
description:
Current doc/HEALTH_DATA.md is wrong on two related points:
- Line saying sleep is 'session envelope only — no stage breakdown'
- Section saying stages are 'not in v4 API at all'

Both contradicted by live probe (2026-05-19): Pixel Watch 3 returns sleep.type=STAGES on 19 of 20 nights in last 30 days, with full per-stage segments and summary.stagesSummary breakdown.

After the sleep-stages sync bead (foodlog-aul) is implemented, update HEALTH_DATA.md to reflect:
1. Sleep stages ARE available via v4 (Sleep.stages array + Sleep.summary.stagesSummary)
2. We now sync per-night DEEP/LIGHT/REM/AWAKE minute totals
3. Update the 'Recorded by Pixel Watch but not in v4 API at all' section to remove sleep stages (keep stress/EDA, Sleep Score, Cardio Load which truly aren't in v4)
4. Optionally add a 'Newly synced (post-foodlog-jav)' table mirroring the existing 'What we currently collect' format

Also: add a reference to docs/health-data-discovery-pixel-watch.md as the source of truth for the v4 catalog audit, and mention the discovery doc URL.

Depends on foodlog-aul being merged first (so the doc reflects what's coded).
close_reason: doc/HEALTH_DATA.md updated to reflect post-foodlog-jav reality: sleep-stages-now-synced corrections, new daily_hrv/daily_sleep_temperature/daily_respiratory_oxygen tables documented, VO2 max marked empty, respiratory-rate-sleep-summary marked skipped-with-reason, activity-level and exercise.splits noted as deferred. Added pointers to discovery-doc URL, findings doc, and v4 quirks memory. Commit 4c62bb2.

title: Sync daily SpO2 + daily respiratory rate (bundled into daily_respiratory_oxygen)
description:
Bundle two low-cost daily signals into one table — second-opinion check against skin-temp illness signal.

New table daily_respiratory_oxygen:
- date (PK)
- breaths_per_min (REAL, from daily-respiratory-rate.breathsPerMinute)
- spo2_avg_pct (REAL, from daily-oxygen-saturation.averagePercentage)
- spo2_low_pct (REAL, from daily-oxygen-saturation.lowerBoundPercentage)
- spo2_high_pct (REAL, from daily-oxygen-saturation.upperBoundPercentage)
- spo2_std_pct (REAL, from daily-oxygen-saturation.standardDeviationPercentage)
- source, external_id

Single _sync_daily_respiratory_oxygen method that hits both endpoints (filter members: daily_oxygen_saturation.date, daily_respiratory_rate.date) and merges by date. Tolerate one endpoint missing on a given day (NaN in the unfilled column).

DO NOT sync respiratory-rate-sleep-summary — has a documented 1000x scale bug (see findings doc §6).

Full schema sketch: docs/health-data-discovery-pixel-watch.md §4.
close_reason: New daily_respiratory_oxygen table merging two v4 endpoints (daily-oxygen-saturation + daily-respiratory-rate) into one date-keyed row. Live: 22 dates over 90d, asymmetric coverage handled correctly (1 resp-only date with NULL spo2 fields). 4 new tests, 185 total pass. Commit 413940c.

title: Sync daily-sleep-temperature-derivations into new daily_sleep_temperature table
description:
Sync the Pixel Watch's nightly skin-temp data — textbook passive flag for illness onset (rises 2-3 days pre-symptom) and alcohol (raises baseline 0.3-0.8 C).

New table daily_sleep_temperature:
- date (PK)
- nightly_temp_c (REAL, from nightlyTemperatureCelsius)
- baseline_temp_c (REAL, from baselineTemperatureCelsius — Google's 30-day median)
- relative_stddev_30d_c (REAL, from relativeNightlyStddev30dCelsius — the 'how unusual is tonight vs. baseline' z-score-like signal)
- source, external_id

Notable: relative_stddev_30d_c is pre-computed by Google. The body-sim 'unusual day' detector can use it directly instead of re-implementing rolling-baseline math.

New client method list_daily_sleep_temperature. Filter: daily_sleep_temperature_derivations.date. Cursor-walk pattern. New _sync method, wired into sync_all.

Tests: parser test against captured live payload, integration test.

Full schema sketch: docs/health-data-discovery-pixel-watch.md §3.
close_reason: New daily_sleep_temperature table + list_daily_sleep_temperature parser + _sync_daily_sleep_temperature (delegating to a new shared _sync_daily_keyed helper) + MCP get_daily_sleep_temperature tool. Live: 22 rows over 90d, ranges sensible. 3 new tests pass, 180 total pass. Commit bfaaabd.

title: Phase 1: Extended Hall body-composition model with population defaults
description:
First phase of the body-composition modeling sub-project. Goal: stand up the full pipeline and scenario simulator using population-default parameters, before any personal fitting. Validates that the model class can roughly track observed weight before we earn the right to personalize.

## Scope
- Data pipeline: SQLite → daily-rollup pandas DataFrame
- HR integration: minute-level HR → Keytel-equation daily activity expenditure; carry hr_data_coverage_pct as a confidence weight
- Macro aggregation per day: protein/carb/fat/sodium → TEF, glycogen-water, sodium-water terms
- Descriptive EDA notebook: weight-vs-glycogen decomposition, missingness map, HR coverage stats, energy balance plots
- Extended Hall baseline notebook (TEF + HR-derived EE + glycogen-water + sodium-water), population-default parameters from literature
- Validation: observed vs predicted weight trajectory on existing data
- Scenario simulator v1 notebook: sliders for intake kcal, macro split, steps, vigorous_min → trajectory bands (uncertainty from population priors, not yet personalized)
- Live tracking notebook scaffold: log scenario predictions weekly so Phase 2 has something to score

## Notebook deliverables
- notebooks/02_data_pipeline.ipynb
- notebooks/03_descriptive_eda.ipynb
- notebooks/04_hall_baseline.ipynb
- notebooks/06_scenario_simulator.ipynb (v1, population params)
- notebooks/07_live_tracking.ipynb (scaffold only)

## Success criteria
- Model runs forward on full date range without errors
- Predicted vs observed weight trajectory plot exists and is sensible (no parameter fit yet — just population defaults)
- Scenario simulator produces uncertainty bands that respond to input changes in physiologically sensible directions
- Live tracking notebook captures a weekly forecast for downstream scoring

## Why this comes first
Phase 2/3/4 personalize parameters via Bayesian inference. That only makes sense once the population-default model is shown to be roughly right. Builds the pipeline that all later phases reuse.

title: MCP list-returning tools render as concatenated objects on mobile clients
description:
Phone Claude rendered get_body_weight output as '{...}{...}' instead of '[{...},{...}]'. Root cause: FastMCP's _convert_to_content (mcp 1.27.0, func_metadata.py) recursively unwraps list/tuple returns into one TextContent block per element with no surrounding array brackets. Desktop clients read structuredContent and reconstruct the array, but mobile Claude appears to render only the unstructured content blocks, concatenating their text.

Affects every list-returning tool in mcp_server/server.py: search_food, log_food, get_entries, get_daily_activity, get_sleep, get_resting_heart_rate, get_workouts, get_body_weight.
design: Change each tool's return shape from 'list[dict]' to a dict wrapper, e.g. {"items": [...]}. That makes the unstructured serialization a single JSON text block. Update tests for the new shape. Verify desktop Claude still works (structuredContent should still validate).
acceptance_criteria: Phone Claude renders get_body_weight result as a single JSON object with an items array. Desktop Claude still works. All 8 affected tools updated consistently. Tests pass.
close_reason: Wrapped 8 list-returning MCP tools as {items:[...]}; verified single-block serialization and full test pass.

title: Expose body weight via MCP
description: Add a fifth read-only health tool, get_body_weight, that returns BodyComposition rows where weight_kg is not null. Default 90-day lookback. Mirrors get_resting_heart_rate / get_workouts pattern. Body fat % deferred.
close_reason: Added get_body_weight MCP tool: 90-day default lookback, weight-only (filters null weight_kg), follows existing health-tool pattern. Tests pass; full suite 164 green.

title: MCP: expose health data (steps, sleep, heart rate, workouts) as read-only tools
description: Currently mcp_server/server.py only exposes food-entry tools. Health data is already synced locally into daily_activity, resting_heart_rate, interval_heart_rate, sleep_sessions, workouts, body_composition tables (via HealthSyncService). Add read-only MCP tools so agents can query these. Coarse-grained tools (get_daily_activity, get_sleep, get_heart_rate, get_workouts) gated on foodlog.read scope, accepting date ranges.
acceptance_criteria: New MCP tools return data from existing health tables; require foodlog.read scope; have unit tests; date-range queries; documented in DASHBOARD.md or equivalent if needed

title: Add cross-view navigation (hamburger menu) between dashboard and timeline
description: Currently the dashboard topbar has a 'Timeline ->' pill that gets you to the timeline, but the timeline topbar has no link back to the daily dashboard. The user has to use the browser back button. Once we have more than two views this will get worse. Add a small navigation control (hamburger icon, dropdown, or persistent nav strip) accessible from every dashboard sub-page so users can switch between Dashboard / Timeline / future views. Should probably live in the topbar to stay out of the chart real estate. Touches: foodlog/templates/base.html (shared nav component), index.html, timeline.html, possibly health_connect.html. Decide pattern: hamburger drawer vs. always-visible pill row vs. tabs.

title: Timeline: landscape rotation works in browser but not in PWA
description: The CSS @media (orientation: landscape) and (max-width: 1024px) rule swaps to single-chart immersive view correctly when the browser is rotated, but the same rotation in the installed PWA does not trigger the swap. Likely cause: PWA viewport is locked to portrait via the manifest, OR the orientation media query needs the user-scalable/viewport meta tag adjusted. Investigate: foodlog/static/manifest.webmanifest 'orientation' field (currently probably 'portrait'), the viewport meta in base.html, and whether iOS Safari handles @media orientation differently in PWA mode vs browser. Repro: install PWA via Add to Home Screen, open /dashboard/timeline, rotate device. Expected: pill switcher + single chart. Actual: stacked panels remain (or layout breaks).

title: Timeline: add proper Y-axis labels with gridlines per chart
description: The current per-panel header shows just 'peak X' or '40-180 bpm' as a single tag. That tells you the bound but not where any specific bar value lands. Need actual Y-axis gridlines (e.g. at 25%/50%/75% of range) with labels on the left edge of each chart so a reader can interpret bar heights numerically. Especially important for HR (fixed 40-180) where a bar at 60% height should obviously read as ~125 bpm. Touches: foodlog/templates/dashboard/timeline_partial.html, foodlog/templates/base.html (CSS for gridlines + axis label column). May want to introduce a small left-side axis-label column inside each .tl-chart container, or use absolute-positioned labels on the chart background.

title: Implement granular timeline view (per spec 2026-05-01)
description: Execute the 23-task implementation plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-foodlog-granular-timeline.md. New /dashboard/timeline page rendering HR + activity intervals at 15-min resolution with workout/meal overlays. Three new tables, three new client iterators, three new sync methods, new router + Jinja partials.
close_reason: All 23 tasks shipped. /dashboard/timeline live with HR range bars + min/max, steps/distance/floors/AZM panels, workout bands, meal dots, date nav, landscape pill switcher, and SSO gating. Real Google data populating: 2071+ HR rows, 868 activity rows, 156 AZM rows. Two integration fixes (pageSize=10000, SSO guard) caught via playwright validation. 151 tests passing.

title: Roll up activity-level samples into daily totals
description:
When phase-2 (Bayesian personalization) begins approaching phase-3 (NEAT/activity-bias work), add daily activity-level totals.

Pixel Watch 3 emits 1-minute activity-level samples with activityLevelType in {SEDENTARY, LIGHTLY_ACTIVE, MODERATELY_ACTIVE, VERY_ACTIVE}. Confirmed via 14-day probe — endpoint live and clean.

DO NOT store per-sample (1440/day = ~43k rows/month uncompressed; storage waste).

Target table daily_activity_minutes:
- date (PK)
- sedentary_min, lightly_min, moderately_min, very_min (INTEGER, each)
- source, external_id

Server-side aggregation in a new client method that paginates 1-min samples and groups by civil date before yielding rows. Filter member: activity_level.interval.civil_start_time.

Defer until the body-sim work actually consumes the NEAT/activity-bias parameters — currently population defaults, not personalized.

title: Persist v4 Google Health API discovery learnings via bd remember
description: Replace any stale reference_google_health_v4_quirks memory with an up-to-date entry covering invariants learned during the foodlog-jav investigation. Run a single bd remember --key google-health-v4-quirks command listing: (1) discovery doc URL is authoritative for type catalog + field schemas; (2) filter grammar uses snake_case data-type prefix even though path is kebab-case and JSON is camelCase; (3) daily-* types support only list+reconcile actions (no rollUp/dailyRollUp); (4) personal-range types documented in DailyRollupDataPoint schema but unreachable — every shape returns INVALID_PARENT_DATA_TYPE_COLLECTION; (5) daily aggregates on Pixel Watch sit at ~60-67% nightly coverage; (6) RespiratoryRateSleepSummary.breathsPerMinute is mis-scaled by 1000x and REM occasionally negative; (7) numeric fields like nonRemHeartRateBeatsPerMinute and averageHeartRateBeatsPerMinute return as string<int64> in JSON. Verify with bd memories google-health.
close_reason: Persisted google-health-v4-quirks memory covering: discovery doc URL, snake_case filter prefix, daily-* list+reconcile only, personal-range unreachable, 60-67% nightly coverage normal, respiratory-rate-sleep-summary 1000x scale bug, string<int64> fields, pointer to docs/health-data-discovery-pixel-watch.md.

title: Investigate Pixel Watch / Google Health Connect data types not yet captured
description:
Discovery task. Determine which additional data types the Pixel Watch exposes via Google Health Connect / Fitbit-backed APIs, assess their availability and quality for this user's account, and produce a findings document so we can decide which (if any) to file as schema/sync enhancement beads.

## Why this matters
The body-composition simulator design (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-18-body-composition-simulator-design.md) lists several known limitations, particularly "model assumes steady-state hormonal context" — i.e. illness, alcohol, severe sleep deprivation are not represented. Several Pixel Watch signals are well-positioned to flag these context shifts, but we don't currently sync them. Before filing implementation beads, we need to know which are actually available with sufficient quality.

## Data types to investigate

### High-value candidates (most likely to address limitations)
1. **HRV — overnight RMSSD** — Health Connect type `HeartRateVariabilityRmssd`
   - Hypothesis: best passive signal for illness, alcohol, training stress, sleep deprivation. Drops 20-50% from baseline in these states.
   - Where it would feed the model: Phase 1 residual diagnostic plot; Phase 4 residual ML candidate feature; possibly a 'trust this day' weight on the likelihood.

2. **Skin temperature variation — overnight** — Health Connect type `SkinTemperature` (Pixel Watch 2 and later)
   - Hypothesis: clearest passive flag for illness onset (often 2-3 days pre-symptom) and alcohol (raises baseline 0.3-0.8 C).
   - Where it would feed the model: same as HRV. Strong candidate for an explicit 'unusual day' indicator.

### Secondary candidates (supporting signals, modest expected impact)
3. **Breathing rate — overnight** — Health Connect type `RespiratoryRate`
   - Redundancy check on illness onset alongside skin temp.

4. **SpO2 — overnight** — Health Connect type `OxygenSaturation`
   - Usually flat 95-99%. Occasional dips could flag sleep-disordered breathing or altitude. Cheap if bundled with breathing rate.

5. **Cardio fitness (VO2 max) estimate** — Fitbit's cardio fitness score
   - Slow-moving. Could refine RMR_scale and activity_bias priors. Updates monthly-ish.

### Probably skip but document why
6. **Daily Readiness Score / Stress Management Score / Cardio Load** — Fitbit composite scores derived from HRV + sleep + activity. Confirm we'd gain nothing by capturing them once we have the underlying signals.

7. **GPS workout traces** — irrelevant for body comp; confirm and dismiss.

8. **Hydration logging** — user-entered, not passive. Document API surface but flag as 'requires behavior change to use'.

## Acceptance criteria

Produce a findings document at `docs/health-data-discovery-pixel-watch.md` containing, for each data type above:

- **Availability** — does Google Health Connect / Fitbit expose it for this user's account today? Confirmed by an actual API probe, not just docs.
- **Data quality** — sample at least 7 days. Coverage rate (what fraction of expected nights/days have a value). Range. Obvious quality issues.
- **API quirks** — any analog of the existing v4 quirks (filter prefix, rollUp behavior, range limits) documented in the persistent memory `reference_google_health_v4_quirks`.
- **Schema sketch** — proposed table or column additions, with the missingness convention (NaN-as-latent vs 0-as-true-zero per the spec).
- **Value assessment** — given the actual quality observed, restate whether this is worth filing as a Phase 1 enhancement bead. Recommend yes / no / bundle-with-X.

Final section of the findings doc: 'Recommended follow-up beads' — a list of titles + one-line descriptions, ready for `bd create`, that we can file (or skip) as a batch.

## Out of scope for this bead
- Implementing any new sync code. Pure investigation.
- Schema migrations. Pure investigation.
- Editing the existing HealthSyncService beyond a read-only probe.

## References
- Existing v4 quirks memory: `bd memories quirks` or `reference_google_health_v4_quirks`
- Existing health-data context: `doc/HEALTH_DATA.md` (if present), `2026-04-22-foodlog-google-health-design.md`
- Spec listing the limitations this is meant to address: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-18-body-composition-simulator-design.md` (Open questions / known limitations section)
close_reason: Investigation complete. Findings at docs/health-data-discovery-pixel-watch.md. Filed follow-up beads: foodlog-aul (sleep stages), foodlog-tat (daily HRV), foodlog-w3y (skin temp), foodlog-iuk (SpO2+resp rate bundle), foodlog-usa (HEALTH_DATA.md correction, depends on -aul), foodlog-1td (persist v4 quirks memory), foodlog-mc9 (activity-level rollup, phase-2 deferred), foodlog-q2k (exercise splits, P4 backlog).

title: Capture sleep stage breakdown (REM, deep, light, awake) in sleep_sessions
description:
The current SleepSession model only stores start_at, end_at, duration_min — total sleep time but no stage breakdown. Both Fitbit and Google Health Connect expose stage-level data (REM / deep / light / awake minutes per session). Capturing it now means the historical series accumulates while we wait for body-sim phases that actually use it.

## Why this matters for body-sim
- **Recovery / training-stress signal:** deep + REM minutes are stronger correlates of perceived recovery than total duration alone. Eventually feeds the residual model in Phase 4.
- **Sleep-quality vs duration disambiguation:** 7h with 90 min deep is very different from 7h with 30 min deep. Treating sleep as a scalar 'hours' hides this.
- **No equation uses it yet** — purely a data-collection enhancement so when Phase 3/4 wants it, the history exists.

## Scope
- Extend SleepSession model with: rem_min, deep_min, light_min, awake_min (all Integer, nullable since older data won't have it)
- Update HealthSyncService to pull stage data from Google Health Connect's sleep API (Fitbit-backed stages exposed via the standard SleepSession resource)
- Backfill not required — null for historical rows is fine
- Update MCP tool get_sleep response schema to include stages when present
- Tests: stage fields populated correctly on new sync; null when source doesn't provide them

## Not blocking Phase 1
The Phase 1 daily-rollup table carries sleep_total_h_prev_night today. Once this bead lands, the table extends to sleep_rem_h / sleep_deep_h / sleep_light_h / sleep_awake_h transparently — no equation changes.

## References
- Discovered during brainstorm on 2026-05-18 — see foodlog-jok Phase 1 epic
- Related: Google Health v4 API quirks (see persistent memory reference_google_health_v4_quirks)

title: Separate consumed_at from logged_at on food_entries
description:
Currently FoodEntry.logged_at and FoodEntry.created_at both default to func.now() at row-write time. There is no way to record 'I ate this at 12:30' vs 'I logged it at 20:00'. For most analyses this is fine, but several body-sim use cases need true consumption time.

## Why this matters for body-sim
- **Glycogen / water dynamics:** the glycogen-water compartment in the Hall-extended model is sensitive to *when* carbs were consumed. A 200g carb dinner has different next-morning weight implications than a 200g carb breakfast.
- **Workout-fueling effects:** protein/carb timing around workouts affects partition fraction p. Currently invisible.
- **Late-eating effects on sleep:** known interaction we'd want to study eventually.
- **Diagnostic 'logging latency':** difference between consumed_at and logged_at correlates with under-reporting risk — strong potential intake_bias signal.

## Scope
- Add `consumed_at` column to FoodEntry model (DateTime, nullable — falls back to logged_at when not provided)
- Update Pydantic schemas to accept optional consumed_at on log_food
- Update MCP tool log_food to accept consumed_at parameter, with reasonable parsing ('30 minutes ago', '12:30', ISO)
- Dashboard timeline view: prefer consumed_at when present
- Tests for parsing, fallback to logged_at when null, dashboard ordering

## Not blocking Phase 1
Phase 1 model proceeds using daily aggregates where intra-day timing doesn't matter. This enhancement unlocks finer-grain analysis in Phase 3/4 and richer descriptive EDA in Phase 1.

## References
- Discovered during brainstorm on 2026-05-18 alongside submission_id enhancement

title: Add submission_id to food_entries for exact batch grouping
description:
The MCP currently writes each food item from a multi-item submission as a separate row in food_entries with auto-incrementing logged_at timestamps (microseconds apart). Inferring 'which items came from the same submission' requires a heuristic time-window cluster (5s in current analysis).

## Why this matters for body-sim
- Batch analysis becomes exact instead of heuristic
- Lets us correlate 'time elapsed since last submission' with logging reliability — a strong potential signal for the intake_bias prior (long gap before logging → more likely something was forgotten)
- Supports a future feature where the MCP/API can flag 'this submission may be incomplete'

## Scope
- Add `submission_id` column to FoodEntry model (e.g., String(64), indexed, nullable for backfill)
- Update MCP tool log_food to generate one submission_id per tool call (UUID or short token)
- Update dashboard /entries router if relevant
- Backfill existing rows by clustering on logged_at within 5s windows (best-effort historical grouping)
- Tests: verify two items logged in one MCP call share submission_id; two items logged separately don't

## Not blocking Phase 1
Phase 1 pipeline can proceed using the 5s-window heuristic. This enhancement makes downstream analysis cleaner and unlocks a real intake-bias prior signal in Phase 2/3.

## References
- Discovered while validating intake_coverage heuristic in brainstorm on 2026-05-18 — see foodlog-jok Phase 1 epic

title: Phase 2: Personalize intake_bias and RMR_scale via Bayesian fit (PyMC)
description:
Second phase of the body-composition modeling sub-project. Goal: replace the two highest-leverage population-default parameters with personalized posteriors fit from the user's accumulated data.

## Why these two parameters first
- intake_bias: systematic under/over-reporting in the food log. Literature shows 20-30% under-reporting is normal; this single parameter usually explains more variance than any other.
- RMR_scale: multiplier on the textbook BMR formula. Genuinely varies ±10% across individuals at the same age/weight/sex.

Other parameters (NEAT_response, protein_protection, activity_bias) are deferred to Phase 3 — they need more data to fit identifiably.

## Trigger
Activate once ~4-6 weeks of additional body-composition readings have accumulated beyond what existed at Phase 1 kickoff (i.e., at least ~30 total weigh-ins with paired food/activity coverage).

## Scope
- notebooks/05_personalize_fit.ipynb (v1 — two parameters)
- PyMC model definition: priors anchored to literature, observed weight as the likelihood target
- NUTS sampling, divergence/Rhat checks
- Posterior plots, posterior-predictive checks
- Update scenario simulator v2 to use posterior samples → narrower trajectory bands
- Re-run live-tracking comparison: did Phase 1 predictions hold up? Did Phase 2 narrow uncertainty appropriately?

## Success criteria
- Fit converges (Rhat < 1.05, ESS adequate, no divergences)
- intake_bias and RMR_scale posteriors are narrower than priors (i.e., data is informative)
- Updated simulator uncertainty bands are tighter than Phase 1 and still cover observed trajectory
- A retrospective on Phase 1 predictions (kept honest by the live-tracking notebook)

## Depends on
Phase 1 milestone complete and pipeline producing the daily-rollup table this notebook consumes.

title: Timeline: stagger overlapping meal dots so each is visually separable
description: When two or more food entries are logged at the same minute (or close enough that their dots visually overlap horizontally), only the top dot is tappable and only one tooltip appears. The user wants to see and tap each meal independently. Reproduce: 2026-04-22 has two lunch entries (Beef Pho + Beef Banh Mi) at the same logged_at, rendered at the same X position so the second is hidden under the first. Approach: when computing meal_views in foodlog/api/routers/timeline.py, detect dots within e.g. <= 1% horizontal distance of each other and assign each a small Y-axis offset (e.g. dot 1 at top:4px, dot 2 at top:14px, etc.) so they stack vertically rather than horizontally. Then expand .tl-meal-strip height to accommodate the tallest stack. Update the rendered button style attribute to include 'top:Npx'. Don't merge dots; keep them individually tappable.

title: Doc: capture Renpho→Fitbit→Google chain and body-fat gap; show weight in lbs(kg)
description: Update doc/HEALTH_DATA.md to (a) document that Renpho data only reaches Google Health via Fitbit relay (Health Connect → Google cloud is not bridged), (b) note the body-fat metric does not survive the Renpho→Fitbit hop. Also update the dashboard Weight card to display lbs primary with kg in parens.
close_reason: Doc update + lbs(kg) UI both shipped; foodlog rebuilt and healthy

title: Document Google Health data coverage matrix
description: Create a doc that maps: (a) what data each connected device (Pixel Watch, Renpho scale) records, (b) what the Google Health v4 API exposes, (c) what FoodLog actually pulls, (d) granularity available vs. granularity stored, (e) sync frequency.
close_reason: Wrote doc/HEALTH_DATA.md covering device → API → FoodLog matrix, granularity gap, and per-type sync cadence.

title: Read exercise.splits / splitSummaries / events if a pace-within-workouts question appears
description: Backlog. The exercise payload already fetched in _sync_workouts_with_hr contains fields we currently ignore: splits, splitSummaries (per-km/mile pace data), exerciseEvents (pause/resume events), exerciseMetadata, activeDuration (duration excluding pauses), notes. Modest value for the body-comp simulator (which only cares about total calories burned and HR aggregates). High value only if a concrete pace-within-walks or training-zone analysis question appears. Defer indefinitely. File this so the field-level gap doesn't get re-rediscovered in a future audit. See docs/health-data-discovery-pixel-watch.md §10.

title: Integrate scenario simulator into foodlog dashboard (/dashboard/simulator)
description:
Move the scenario simulator from the Jupyter notebook deliverable (Phase 1, notebook 06_scenario_simulator.ipynb) into the FastAPI dashboard as /dashboard/simulator. Lets the user explore 'what if' scenarios from any device including phone, without running Jupyter.

## Why this is Phase 3 and not earlier
- The notebook simulator already covers the exploration workflow during model development. Building a polished UI for a model that's actively being refit weekly is wasted polish.
- Only after Phase 3 (NEAT, protein_protection, activity_bias personalized) is the model trustworthy enough to deserve a production surface.
- Requires the Phase 1/2/3 validation thresholds to have passed (>=80% of weigh-ins inside 95% band, MAE < 1.0 kg, no residual drift). Without that, exposing a simulator UI promises more confidence than the model has.

## Scope
- New FastAPI router: foodlog/api/routers/simulator.py
- New Jinja template: foodlog/api/templates/simulator.html (HTMX + Notion-system per DESIGN.md)
- Server-side: load latest posterior samples from a persisted artifact (numpy/parquet)
- Inputs: intake_kcal, macro split, steps, vigorous_min, horizon_days — same as notebook 06
- Outputs: predicted weight + bf% trajectory with 95% band, derived 'fat lost' / 'lean change' summary
- Gated behind SSO (same auth as rest of dashboard)
- Caching: posterior samples don't change between fits; cache reads aggressively

## Out of scope for this bead
- Model retraining triggered from UI (always done offline from a notebook)
- Multi-user (foodlog remains single-user)
- Saving/sharing scenarios

## Depends on
Phase 3 milestone (foodlog-6bi) complete. May be reconsidered after Phase 2 if calibration is already excellent.

title: Phase 4 (optional): ML residual model on top of mechanistic baseline
description:
Fourth and optional phase. Once the personalized mechanistic model is mature, train an ML model to predict the residuals (observed - mechanistic-predicted weight) from features the mechanistic model doesn't capture. Decide empirically whether the residuals are signal or noise; if noise, skip this phase entirely.

## Candidate residual features
- Sleep duration and timing (modulates recovery, NEAT, hunger)
- RHR drift over trailing 3-7 days (signal of accumulated training stress, illness, underrecovery)
- Day-of-week, weekend effects
- vigorous_min as a non-linear EPOC driver beyond what TEF/Hall handles

## Trigger
Activate once ~6 months of data has accumulated AND the mechanistic model's residuals show structure (not white noise). Run a residual autocorrelation + feature-correlation check before committing to this phase.

## Scope
- notebooks/08_residual_ml.ipynb
- Residual diagnostics first: autocorrelation, partial dependence on candidate features
- Only proceed to model fitting if residual structure exists
- XGBoost (or Ridge on engineered lag features) trained with time-series CV
- Compare hybrid predictions (mechanistic + ML residual) vs mechanistic-only on a held-out window

## Success criteria
- Hybrid model meaningfully reduces error on held-out predictions vs mechanistic-only — OR explicit decision to skip with documented reasoning
- No degradation of the mechanistic model's counterfactual validity (the ML layer only adjusts predictions, never overwrites the energy-balance core)

## Depends on
Phase 3 milestone complete. May be cancelled if residual analysis shows mechanistic model is already accurate to noise floor.

title: Phase 3: Add NEAT_response, protein_protection, activity_bias to Bayesian fit
description:
Third phase. Extends the personalized fit beyond intake_bias and RMR_scale to the remaining mechanistically meaningful parameters. Each is added sequentially with an identifiability check before adopting.

## Parameters being added
- NEAT_response: how much spontaneous non-exercise activity adapts to over/under-eating. Hard to fit without long deficit/surplus periods.
- protein_protection: how strongly adequate protein intake (>1.6 g/kg) modifies the Forbes partition fraction during caloric deficit. Requires variation in protein intake to identify.
- activity_bias: scaling between HR-Keytel-derived expenditure and true activity calories. Requires both watch-worn and watch-not-worn segments to identify.

## Trigger
Activate once ~3 months of data has accumulated since project start. Each parameter is added only when the conditions exist in the data for it to be identifiable.

## Scope
- notebooks/05_personalize_fit.ipynb (v2 — sequential parameter addition)
- Identifiability check before adopting each new parameter: posterior should be informative relative to prior, posterior-predictive should improve, no parameter drift induced in already-fit parameters
- Updated scenario simulator using full personalized posterior
- Retrospective on Phase 1/2 prediction tracking

## Success criteria
- At least one of the three additional parameters fits identifiably and improves posterior-predictive accuracy
- Document which parameters are *not* yet identifiable from current data and what conditions would change that

## Depends on
Phase 2 milestone complete.

value: Ryan's FoodLog PWA runs on Android, not iOS. Relevant when discussing PWA install/refresh flows: Android uses Chrome's Install app / Add to Home Screen, manifest cache is per-Chrome-profile, force-stop alone doesn't always refresh the manifest — clear app data or uninstall+reinstall the shortcut to guarantee a fresh manifest pickup.

Google Health v4 API quirks and invariants (compiled 2026-05-19 from foodlog-jav investigation, supersedes any earlier 'reference_google_health_v4_quirks' memory):

(1) DISCOVERY DOC IS AUTHORITATIVE. https://health.googleapis.com/$discovery/rest?version=v4 — full machine-readable catalog of every data type, every field, every type signature. Always read it before guessing endpoint or field names.

(2) FILTER GRAMMAR USES SNAKE_CASE TYPE PREFIX. URL path is kebab-case (daily-heart-rate-variability) and JSON fields are camelCase (dailyHeartRateVariability), but the filter expression uses the snake_case form (daily_heart_rate_variability.date >= '2026-05-01'). The single source of truth in code is FILTER_FIELDS in foodlog/clients/google_health.py.

(3) DAILY-* TYPES ARE LIST+RECONCILE ONLY. daily-heart-rate-variability, daily-resting-heart-rate, daily-oxygen-saturation, daily-respiratory-rate, daily-sleep-temperature-derivations all reject :rollUp and :dailyRollUp with UNSUPPORTED_DATA_TYPE_ACTION. Use plain :list with a date filter and cursor-walk on date.

(4) PERSONAL-RANGE TYPES ARE DOCUMENTED BUT UNREACHABLE. DailyRollupDataPoint schema lists heartRateVariabilityPersonalRange and restingHeartRatePersonalRange. In practice every probe shape returns INVALID_PARENT_DATA_TYPE_COLLECTION on heart-rate-variability-personal-range / resting-heart-rate-personal-range. Compute personal baselines locally (rolling 30-day median + IQR over daily_hrv / resting_heart_rate).

(5) PIXEL WATCH NIGHTLY COVERAGE IS ~60-67%. All daily-aggregate types (HRV, SpO2, resp rate, skin temp, sleep stages) sit at this rate over 30 days. Tied to wear-all-night completeness. Treat NaN-days as 'no signal,' not as outliers — body-sim 'unusual day' detector should skip those days, not score them as zero.

(6) RESPIRATORY-RATE-SLEEP-SUMMARY HAS A 1000X UNIT BUG. Reported breathsPerMinute values are ~0.01; multiply by 1000 to match daily-respiratory-rate. REM stats occasionally negative (sentinel for 'no REM detected'). Skip this endpoint; use daily-respiratory-rate.

(7) INTEGER FIELDS COME BACK AS STRINGS. nonRemHeartRateBeatsPerMinute, averageHeartRateBeatsPerMinute, and other string<int64> fields per discovery doc — cast at parse time. This bit us on workouts before; bites again on daily HRV.

(8) FULL CATALOG AUDIT AT docs/health-data-discovery-pixel-watch.md. Per-type availability/quality/quirks/schema sketch/value assessment, plus the recommended bead list. Source of truth when planning new sync work.
